query: Onion skin appearance in renal biopsy

retrieved_docs.title: Systemic Sclerosis (Scleroderma) -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Renal involvement in systemic sclerosis is relatively less common. Chronic ischemic changes are frequently observed, although glomerulonephritis is uncommon unless there is an overlapping syndrome. Scleroderma renal crisis, a rare yet life-threatening manifestation of systemic sclerosis, is associated with changes resembling those seen in thrombotic microangiopathy or malignant hypertension. [22] Histologically, onion skinning (intimal proliferation and reduplication of the elastic lamina with luminal narrowing) of the small interlobular and arcuate renal arteries is a characteristic finding in such cases.

retrieved_docs.title: Scleroderma and Renal Crisis -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Myxoid intimal proliferation with luminal narrowing occurs early and as the lesions progress, smooth muscle cell infiltration and deposition of collagen occurs in the intima, resulting in the appearance of concentric fibrosis or “onion skin” appearance. Superimposed fibrin thrombi and fibrinoid necrosis of the media are also seen. [1] [2] [6] [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Thrombotic Microangiopathy and the Kidney -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: With chronicity, there is duplication or “double contouring” of the glioblastoma from new subendothelial basement membrane formation. This appears as a membranoproliferative pattern without immune deposits and requires further examination by immunofluorescence and electron microscopy. Additionally, an “onion-skinning” appearance of interlobular and arcuate arterioles may form from myointimal hyperplasia. [12] Immunofluorescence will be negative for immunoglobulin or complement staining. Electron microscopy may show subendothelial electron-lucent material corresponding to the "double contours" seen in light microscopy; electron-dense deposits should not be seen. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Pericholangitis -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Liver biopsy - The most specific histologic finding in SDPSC is the "onion skin" pattern, which describes as fibrous obliteration and concentric replacement of connective tissue in small bile ducts. [20] Although, this classic finding histology alone is not enough for making a diagnosis of SDPSC. [18] Liver ultrasound - Ultrasound findings are commonly used to rule out a secondary cause of chronic cholestasis, such as pancreatic disease and gallbladder abnormalities. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Pericholangitis -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: Except for extrahepatic PSC, LDPSC, SDPSC, and intrahepatic PSC have similar liver histology features, described as nonsuppurative cholangitis, periductal fibrosis, ductular reaction, and ductopenia. However, in extrahepatic PSC, the histology features are nondiagnostic or nonspecific features of cholestasis, particularly in early-stage disease. [14] Liver Biopsy findings classically reported as an “onion skin” appearance, which includes non-suppurative cholangitis, ductular reaction, ductopenia, and periductal fibrosis. However, this classic finding is present in almost 10% of liver biopsies. Furthermore, histology alone is not enough for making the diagnosis of PSC. [18] Other diseases such as IgG4-associated cholangiopathy, ductopenic rejection following a liver transplant, and acquired immune deficiency syndrome also can have similar histopathology, which seen in PSC. [19] The main reason for liver biopsy in large-duct PSC patients is to exclude other diseases, determine the stage of PSC, and diagnose small-duct PSC. [20]

retrieved_docs.title: Jessner Lymphocytic Infiltration of the Skin  -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Basic laboratory studies, including complete blood count and urinalysis, should be done.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Dermis -- Microscopy, Light
retrieved_docs.content: Hair follicles, glands, and ducts can be seen throughout the epidermis, dermis, and hypodermis. A higher magnification view can further distinguish sebaceous, apocrine, and eccrine sweat glands. Meissner's corpuscles and Pacinian corpuscles can also be distinguished by light microscopy. Pacinian corpuscles in the deep dermis or hypodermis have an onion-like appearance on cross-sectional views. Meissner corpuscles are seen in the dermal papillae as oblong structures with neurons in a spiral orientation surrounded by a fibrous capsule. [10] Polarizing light microscopy is useful in the diagnosis of depositional diseases such as amyloidosis and gout. Tissue samples stained with Congo red dye may be examined under polarized light, producing a characteristic apple-green birefringence of protein deposits in amyloidosis. [15] Similarly, polarized light microscopy is used to differentiate urate crystal and calcium deposition. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Nephrogenic Systemic Fibrosis -- History and Physical -- Dermatologic
retrieved_docs.content: The affected areas have been described as hard and "woody." The plaques are usually symmetrical and start on the hands or feet and progress medially (in contrast, scleroderma usually starts on the trunk and progresses distally). The Rodnan skin thickness score can measure skin thickness, and skin is often discolored to a brawny color. The skin can have a peau d'orange appearance (ie, induration overlying hard subcutaneous tissue). The face is almost always spared. [1] [12]

retrieved_docs.title: Erythema Multiforme -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: A skin biopsy of the lesion center can be performed for histological study with immunofluorescence, as it shows epithelial intercellular edema with keratinocyte necrosis, which is responsible for an intraepidermal or subepidermal blister covered with a necrotic epidermis. A perivascular lymphohistiocytic infiltrate is present in the superficial dermis without necrotic vascular lesions. Direct immunofluorescence is negative. The biological assessment provides no argument for the diagnosis of erythema multiforme. However, it is helpful to appreciate the severity of the disease. Renal, hepatic, or hematologic lesions have also been described and are not systematically sought after in mild forms. [28]

retrieved_docs.title: Ultrasound of the Urinary Tract -- Technique or Treatment -- Scrotum, Testicles, and Epididymis
retrieved_docs.content: Although they can mimic testicular cancers on imaging, the lack of tumor markers, the characteristic onion skin appearance on ultrasound, and the lack of internal vascularity on color Doppler imaging are typically sufficient to make the diagnosis. [261]

retrieved_docs.title: Renal Biopsy -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Patients with difficult-to-reach targets are served best by coordinating the biopsy so that both ultrasound and CT can be used as necessary to afford the advantages of both modalities, such as the speed of ultrasound, tissue contrast, and resolution of CT. CT-guided biopsies of upper pole lesions protected by ribs may be made feasible only through CT gantry angling or trigonometric analysis to estimate the necessary insertion angle. The angle at which to direct the needle concerning the level of the table can be calculated by first dividing the depth of the lesion by the distance of the lesion from the skin entry point and then by calculating the inverse tangent of that number.

retrieved_docs.title: Goodpasture Syndrome -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: The biopsy tissue should be analyzed using light, electron, and immunofluorescence microscopy. Light microscopy usually shows proliferative or necrotizing crescentic glomerulopathy, as described above. The crescents become fibrotic over time, leading to the development of frank glomerulosclerosis, tubular atrophy, and interstitial fibrosis. [19]

retrieved_docs.title: Ultrasound of the Urinary Tract -- Technique or Treatment -- Scrotum, Testicles, and Epididymis
retrieved_docs.content: Epidermoid cysts are rare, benign testicular neoplasms that are characterized by multiple concentric layers of keratinizing squamous epithelium and keratinous debris, giving them a solid hypoechoic appearance. [261] [262] These cysts are typically round with concentric hyperechoic layers internally, giving them an onion skin appearance, and lack detectable internal vascular flow on color Doppler ultrasonography. [261] [262] Although they are the most common benign neoplasm of the testes, they only account for 1% to 2% of all testicular masses. [261] [263] [264]

retrieved_docs.title: Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The reported mortality rate of TEN cases is 25 to 30% compared to 1 to 5% in SJS cases. The prognosis of TEN cases depends on the degree of cutaneous affection. [47] The higher the affected body surface area, the higher the mortality rate. [26] [48] Infection is the main cause of death. Other potential lethal complications are adult respiratory distress syndrome, pulmonary embolism, renal and cardiac failure, and gastrointestinal bleeding. [49]

retrieved_docs.title: Blister Agents -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Blister agents, such as sulfur mustard (dichlorethylsulphide) and nitrogen mustard, have been utilized in warfare since antiquity, particularly during the First and Second World Wars. Recognized by their distinct odor reminiscent of onions, horseradish, or garlic, these agents induce blistering of the skin and mucous membranes upon exposure. The resultant burns are not only excruciating but also disfiguring, with potential complications including delayed wound healing, scarring, and increased susceptibility to secondary infections. Eye contact with these agents can lead to conjunctivitis, corneal damage, and blindness. Furthermore, respiratory complications ranging from inflammation and coughing to bronchitis, pneumonia, and respiratory failure can manifest, particularly with prolonged or high-dose exposure. In light of these hazards, treatment primarily involves supportive measures and necessitates specialized care at burn centers for optimal wound management and, if necessary, skin grafting.

retrieved_docs.title: Goodpasture Syndrome -- Histopathology
retrieved_docs.content: In patients suspected to have Goodpasture syndrome, a renal biopsy should be considered. A kidney biopsy is not only crucial for diagnosis, but the percentage of crescents identified on biopsy confers renal survival prognosis. Lung tissue is typically not sought due to the invasiveness of the procedure and a more difficult immunofixation process for lung tissue.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 30 -- Level 4 -- Community 2732
retrieved_docs.content: Other specified conditions related to pregnancy comprise a range of distinct gestational disorders. This category includes renal disorder during pregnancy, peripheral neuropathy during pregnancy, and polymorphic eruption of pregnancy, each representing a separate organ‑system complication that can arise in the context of gestation and is classified under the broader label of other specified pregnancy‑related conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2623
retrieved_docs.content: Necrotizing glomerulonephritis is closely linked to داء مضاد للغشاء القاعدي الكبيبي, an anti‑basement membrane disease of the kidney, and it also forms a key component of متلازمة رئية كلوية, a pulmonary‑renal syndrome that involves simultaneous injury to the lungs and kidneys. This interrelationship highlights the importance of recognizing necrotizing glomerulonephritis not only as a severe renal pathology but also as a manifestation of systemic autoimmune processes that can affect multiple organ systems, guiding comprehensive diagnostic evaluation and coordinated therapeutic strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2863
retrieved_docs.content: Malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis is clinically similar to hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension, indicating that the severe renal vascular changes seen in malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis share comparable clinical manifestations, risk factors, and disease mechanisms with complicated primary hypertension and hypertension that arises secondary to other underlying conditions. This similarity underscores the importance of recognizing overlapping signs and symptoms when diagnosing and managing patients with advanced hypertensive organ damage and secondary causes of elevated blood pressure.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 1927
retrieved_docs.content: The urinary system is identified as the anatomic site associated with الدَّاءُ السِّيسْتينِي, indicating that this disease primarily involves or impacts the structures of the urinary tract. Recognizing this relationship emphasizes the importance of evaluating urinary system function when diagnosing or managing الدَّاءُ السِّيسْتينِي, as the disease’s manifestations are closely linked to the urinary organs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2842
retrieved_docs.content: Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis and essential hypertension are clinically similar conditions, as both involve pathological changes in small arterial vessels that lead to elevated blood pressure and related end‑organ effects. Recognizing the overlap in their clinical presentations helps clinicians differentiate primary hypertension from secondary renal vascular disease, guides appropriate diagnostic evaluation, and informs management strategies aimed at controlling blood pressure and preserving kidney function.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2554
retrieved_docs.content: Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis is classified as a subtype of اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية (renal vascular disorder) and shares clinical characteristics with essential hypertension, indicating that patients with this form of arteriolar kidney disease often present with blood‑pressure patterns and target‑organ effects similar to those seen in essential hypertension. Recognizing this relationship helps clinicians differentiate primary hypertensive disease from secondary renal vascular pathology and guides appropriate diagnostic evaluation and management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2449
retrieved_docs.content: The Vascular System is identified as the anatomical site linked to both اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية (renal vascular disorder) and تسلخ الشرايين (arterial erosion). These conditions involve pathological changes within the blood vessels, with اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية affecting the renal vasculature and تسلخ الشرايين involving damage to arterial walls. Recognizing that both disorders share the Vascular System as their underlying anatomic substrate underscores the central role of vascular health in the development and management of renal vascular disease and arterial erosion, and highlights the need for comprehensive vascular assessment and care in patients presenting with these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 2181
retrieved_docs.content: The Vascular System serves as the anatomic site associated with several distinct vascular disorders. It is linked to “other and ill-defined cerebrovascular disease,” reflecting a range of poorly characterized conditions affecting the brain’s blood vessels. It is also associated with “اضطراب الأوعية الكلوية,” a renal vessel disorder that involves abnormalities of the kidney’s vascular network. Additionally, the Vascular System is connected to “تسلخ الشرايين,” a condition characterized by arterial ulceration or erosion. Recognizing that these diverse pathologies—cerebrovascular disease, renal vascular disorder, and arterial ulceration—all involve the Vascular System underscores the importance of comprehensive vascular assessment in patient evaluation and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1795
retrieved_docs.content: The skin disorder حُمامَى is linked to other inflammatory conditions of the skin, indicating that it shares pathogenic mechanisms and clinical associations with additional inflammatory dermatoses. retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 2 -- Community 921
retrieved_docs.content: Essential hypertension is identified and interpreted through increased blood pressure measurements, which are central to its clinical assessment. Benign arteriolar nephrosclerosis exhibits clinical features that are similar to those of essential hypertension, highlighting overlapping manifestations and the need to consider both conditions when evaluating patients with elevated blood pressure and related renal changes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 4 -- Community 2867
retrieved_docs.content: Overige gespecificeerde aandoeningen verband houdend met zwangerschap, a category denoting other specified conditions related to pregnancy, is mapped to اضطراب كلوي أثناء الحمل, indicating a kidney disorder that can occur during gestation, and is also mapped to طفح الحمل متعدد الأشكال, describing a multiform rash that may appear in pregnant individuals. Recognizing that these renal and dermatologic complications are encompassed within the broader classification of other specified pregnancy‑related conditions helps clinicians identify and manage such manifestations effectively during patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ and ربط البوق are medical concepts that frequently co‑occur, indicating that the presence of حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ is often associated with the finding or procedure ربط البوق. This consistent association suggests that clinicians should be aware of the link between these two entities, as recognizing one may prompt evaluation for the other, thereby informing diagnosis and guiding appropriate patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2356

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1847
retrieved_docs.content: Hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension is interpreted as ضغط الدم (blood pressure), emphasizing that this complex form of high blood pressure is fundamentally a disorder of blood pressure regulation. ضغط الدم is directly related to ضغط الدم المنخفض (low blood pressure), indicating that the spectrum of blood pressure abnormalities—from elevated to reduced levels—are interconnected clinical entities. Malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis is clinically similar to hypertension with complications and secondary hypertension, reflecting that severe arteriolar damage in the kidneys can present with features indistinguishable from complicated or secondary forms of hypertension. Together, these relationships underscore the importance of evaluating both high and low blood pressure states and recognizing their potential overlap with serious renal vascular pathology such as malignant arteriolar nephrosclerosis for accurate diagnosis and optimal patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1550
retrieved_docs.content: Diseases and injuries are classified as a subcategory of اضطراب بولي تناسلي (genitourinary disorder) and are also placed under the broader heading of signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions, indicating that many disease and injury entities within the genitourinary system present with nonspecific clinical manifestations. The term Symptoms is described as closely related to, and possibly synonymous with, signs and symptoms of ill‑defined conditions, emphasizing that what clinicians label as symptoms often overlap with the vague or poorly defined signs that accompany various diseases and injuries. This hierarchical relationship highlights the interconnected nature of disease classification, symptom terminology, and the challenge of identifying precise clinical features in genitourinary and other poorly defined conditions, which is important for accurate diagnosis and patient management.
